methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $40M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

data.narrative:
Solinftec is a Series B robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $40M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 9 private frontier-tech peers in robotics and manufacturing, including 1X Technologies, Bedrock Robotics, Dexterity, Collaborative Robotics, and Fairmat, alongside one public comparable.

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression method, anchoring to $40M total raised and applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, implies a valuation of $76M. The peer-median method across six same-sector, same-stage companies yields a median valuation of $1.1B, with a wide range from $300M to $10.0B reflecting substantial dispersion in the robotics and manufacturing sector at this stage.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $65M (low) to $1.2B (high), with a median estimate of $576M. The wide dispersion between stage-progression and peer-median results reflects structural uncertainty in the frontier-tech robotics sector, where valuations at Series B vary materially based on product maturity, market traction, and capital intensity. The peer range itself—spanning nearly two orders of magnitude—underscores that stage alone does not determine valuation in this space. Key uncertainties include Solinftec's position within the maturity spectrum of its peer set and the sustainability of current sector valuations in manufacturing automation.
